steps.title: Spot the parallel dependence
steps.content: The passage draws a parallel: the flower flourishes when “the dew and the rain draw it forth” and languishes without them, just as the mind flourishes when “fed with the sweet dew of the fostering spirit” and languishes “Lacking this.” The key signal is this structure showing mutual dependence on nourishment.

steps.title: Match the core likeness
steps.content:
Both require nourishment to thrive—that's what C states.
  • A introduces time (never mentioned)
  • B conflates metaphorical “dew of spirit” with literal weather changes
  • D claims perseverance despite the passage showing languishing without nourishment
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
